name: Hwang Jini
description: Hwang Jini was a legendary gisaeng (female entertainer) who lived during the Joseon Dynasty of Korea, likely in the 16th century. Born into a noble family but relegated to the gisaeng class due to her mother's status, she was renowned for her exceptional beauty, sharp wit, profound poetry, and mastery of music and dance. More than just an entertainer, Hwang Jini was a cultural icon who challenged societal norms and prejudices through her art and relationships. She captivated the most brilliant minds of her time, including scholars, aristocrats, and monks, often testing their intellect and virtue. Her poems, written in both Korean and classical Chinese, explored themes of love, nature, and the human condition, revealing a deep understanding of the world. She is remembered not only for her artistic talents but also for her independent spirit and her ability to transcend the limitations imposed on women in her era. Hwang Jini's legacy continues to inspire artists and writers, solidifying her place as a prominent figure in Korean history and literature. She represents a complex and captivating figure who embodies both beauty and intellectual prowess.
subtitle: Legendary Gisaeng, Poet, and Iconoclast

keyMyths.title: Tempting the Monk
keyMyths.description: Hwang Jini is said to have tempted the venerable monk Ji Un-su, who vowed to remain celibate for 30 years. She tested his resolve, and he eventually broke his vow.

keyMyths.title: Challenging the Scholar
keyMyths.description: A scholar, Byeokgye Su-dong, boasted he would not be swayed by her charms. Hwang Jini then deliberately seduced him, proving her allure and challenging his arrogance.

keyMyths.title: Her Poetry's Power
keyMyths.description: Hwang Jini's poems are still read and admired today, demonstrating her literary skill and her ability to capture the nuances of human emotion and the beauty of the natural world.
keyMyths.source: Collected works of Hwang Jini's poetry

sources.author: Kenneth Rexroth
sources.work: One Hundred More Poems from the Japanese
sources.description: Includes translations of Hwang Jini's poems.
sources.text: Includes translations of Hwang Jini's poems.
